message:
chore(beads): add bd-006e (case-insensitive label matching) and rotate history

- Add new issue bd-006e: make TUI and label attention case-insensitive
- Rotate .br_history: remove 20260323 snapshots, add 20260327 snapshots
- Update issues.jsonl with new issue entry

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -1,3 +1,4 @@
+{"id":"bd-006e","title":"Make TUI and label attention case-insensitive for label matching","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-27T05:04:52.663850747Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-27T05:05:02.129169095Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-0loi","title":"Epic: Make all version strings dynamic and self-consistent","description":"Currently, 5+ locations in the codebase hard-coded version strings like 1.0.0, 0.1.0, or 1.0 instead of deriving from CARGO_PKG_VERSION. This session fixed the known instances (commit 12a6401), but the underlying pattern needs a systematic guard to prevent regression. When the package version in Cargo.toml changes (e.g., 0.1.0 to 0.2.0), hard-coded version strings silently become wrong. For a triage engine where determinism and trust are core values, emitting incorrect version metadata undermines the tool credibility. This epic covers adding CI-level guards and documentation to prevent future regressions.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:04:57.054022207Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:16:12.440985161Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T03:16:12.440412838Z","close_reason":"All subtasks complete: CI version guard (bd-0loi.1) and blurb version docs (bd-0loi.2)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["robustness","versioning"]}
 {"id":"bd-0loi.1","title":"Add grep-based CI check that catches hard-coded version strings","description":"Add a CI step (or a cargo test) that greps the src/ directory for literal version strings that should instead use env!(CARGO_PKG_VERSION). The check should flag patterns like '"1.0.0"' or '"0.1.0"' in non-test Rust code that appear in version-like contexts (struct fields named 'version', 'schema_version', etc.).

IMPLEMENTATION APPROACH:
- Preferred: a #[test] function in lib.rs or a dedicated test file that uses include_str! or reads src/ files and asserts no hard-coded version patterns exist outside of test code
- Alternative: a shell script in scripts/ invoked by CI that greps for suspicious patterns
- The test should allowlist known safe uses (e.g., the BLURB_VERSION HTML markers in agents.rs which are guarded by the agent_blurb_version_matches_constant test)

WHY THIS MATTERS:
Five instances of hard-coded versions were found and fixed in commit 12a6401. Without a guard, the same mistake will recur the next time someone adds a new struct with a version field. The robot envelope, pages meta, triage meta, and schema structs all need version fields — its easy to forget env!() when writing new code.

FILES TO CHECK:
- src/analysis/triage.rs (TriageMeta.version)
- src/robot.rs (RobotSchemas.schema_version, RobotEnvelope.version)
- src/export_pages.rs (PagesMeta.version)
- src/analysis/recipe.rs (default_version())
- src/analysis/brief.rs (BriefMeta.version)
- Any new file that adds a version/schema_version field","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":45,"created_at":"2026-03-25T03:05:13.274235658Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:15:19.386395208Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T03:15:19.385413951Z","close_reason":"Version guard test added in lib.rs (commit 86593df)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ci","robustness","versioning"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-0loi.1","depends_on_id":"bd-0loi","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:05:13.274235658Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-0loi.2","title":"Extract BLURB_VERSION into the agent blurb marker string dynamically","description":"In agents.rs, BLURB_VERSION (line 15) is a u32 constant set to 1, but the AGENT_BLURB raw string literal (line 25) hard-codes the marker as '<!-- bv-agent-instructions-v1 -->'. When BLURB_VERSION is bumped to 2, someone must manually update the marker inside the raw string — which is easy to forget.

CURRENT STATE:
- A guard test (agent_blurb_version_matches_constant) was added in commit 12a6401 that will catch the mismatch at test time
- But the ideal fix is to construct the blurb dynamically so the version cant drift

CHALLENGE:
AGENT_BLURB is a const &str (raw string literal), which cant contain format!() or concat!(). Options:
1. Use lazy_static! or std::sync::LazyLock to construct the blurb at runtime with the version interpolated
2. Use a const fn that concatenates using const_format crate
3. Use a build.rs step that generates the blurb with the correct version
4. Keep the current approach (const raw string + guard test) and accept the manual sync — the test makes it safe

RECOMMENDATION:
Option 4 (keep guard test) is simplest and already implemented. Only upgrade to option 1/2/3 if the version needs to change frequently. Document the coupling in a code comment near BLURB_VERSION.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":30,"created_at":"2026-03-25T03:05:28.086663623Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:16:11.834277183Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T03:16:11.833575955Z","close_reason":"Documented coupling with comments on BLURB_VERSION and AGENT_BLURB, guarded by existing test (commit d738f1b)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["robustness","versioning"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-0loi.2","depends_on_id":"bd-0loi","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:05:28.086663623Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-0loi.2","depends_on_id":"bd-0loi.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:08:46.792336461Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
